tipRules.tip: KVD: Texas rig 5-inch Ocho in blue craw or green pumpkin — pitch into holes in the grass at 9-11ft. 3/8oz tungsten with 3/0 Mustad grip pin hook. Color doesn't matter much — 'anything green pumpkin.' The Ocho just glides straight down into the clean holes.

KVD: When it goes slick calm and fish get finicky, switch the same Ocho to a wacky rig on a spinning rod. Cast it out, let it painfully sink to the bottom, twitch a few times — they usually eat it before it hits bottom.

knowledge.insight: KVD: On Cayuga Lake (northern natural lake), the north end is a huge expanse of flats at 8-12ft, all grass. The key is finding clean holes in the grass at 9-11ft. Good holes have a little bit of rock in them. Each hole has its own ecosystem — bluegill, perch, gobies all live there. Good holes have reloaded with fish all week.

KVD: For pitching into grass holes — 3/8oz tungsten weight with a 3/0 Mustad grip pin hook (the pin keeps the bait from sliding down). 5-inch Ocho worm. Blue craw or green pumpkin. Making it fall straight is critical — the Texas rig glides down clean into the holes.
